_meta.title: Anycubic (Kobra) — control verb catalog (verb → channel / params)
_meta.transport: MQTT over TLS :9883 (+ plain-HTTP :18910 for the file upload leg)

derived_from.section: Writing / control + Reading state (query set) + Print launch
derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Anycubic's own SlicerNext (OrcaSlicer fork) device layer + sanitized owner captures (12 commands captured byte-for-byte on Kobra 3). No code copied; no secrets.

_meta.safety: ⚠️ Control writes drive a hot, moving machine. Gate every write behind an explicit 'enable writes' and validate against your own device. A 🟢 read fact is low-risk; a 🟢 control fact is not.
notes: Each command is `{type, action}` in the PAYLOAD, not the topic — see ./topic-grammar.json rule 'action-in-payload'.
Wire spelling is load-bearing: the verb is `tempature` (sic) and `fan:setSpeed` (not `fan:set`).
`data` params are only listed where the source paper states them; elsewhere `data` is marked not-enumerated (validate live before shipping).

correlation: Echoed `msgid`; a bare `{"msgid": ...}` ACK returns on a `.../response` topic. An ACK proves the command was received, not that the effect landed — confirm via the next report.

commands.purpose: Launch a print (see print_launch — HTTP upload precedes this).

commands.note: Verb is misspelled 'tempature' on the wire — match exactly.
Feeder verb; the ACE (multiColorBox) read shape is documented in the paper's Multi-material section.

commands.purpose: Set the single external-spool feeder's loaded material + color.
commands.note: Feeder verb; the single external spool (sibling to multiColorBox). Its reported `loaded` flag doubles as the runout signal — see the paper's Multi-material / feeders section. 🟢 hardware-validated.

queries.description: Read-only poll set — an idle printer emits nothing unsolicited (poll ~48 s baseline); a printing one auto-pushes `tempature` + `print`.

print_launch.description: Two-step: HTTP upload, then MQTT print:start referencing the uploaded file.

fields.gcode: the file bytes (must be a real .gcode.3mf)

steps.note: avata /info omits fileUploadurl — take it from the MQTT report's urls.fileUploadurl; gkapi advertises it.
